questions: Using the '7 Stages of Film Production' framework as a guide, identify the two specialized schools at the New York Film Academy (NYFA) that correspond to: (1) the creator responsible for the 'Development' phase's final written deliverable, and (2) the professional role that 'supervises and manages' the entire lifecycle including financing, marketing, and distribution. For both identified schools, list the names of the faculty members explicitly mentioned in their program overviews and provide a comprehensive list of all their 'Online' workshop offerings, including the duration and specific title of each.

Identify the two specific academic divisions or programs at the New York Film Academy (NYFA) that correspond to the 'Development' phase (script creation) and the 'Financing/Marketing' phases (entrepreneurial producing), specifically those led by Dean Nunzio DeFilippis and Chair Neal Weisman respectively. For the script-focused department associated with DeFilippis, list three distinct genres or formats explicitly offered as 'part-time' workshops. For the producing-focused online master's program associated with Weisman, report the tuition cost per semester, the program's total duration, and three specific examples of 'hands-on' courses listed in the curriculum.

rounds.answer_a:
The two specialized schools at NYFA corresponding to these film production roles are the **Screenwriting School** and the **Producing School**.

The Screenwriting School corresponds to the Development phase's final written deliverable - the screenplay. Faculty members explicitly mentioned include Peter Katz, who serves as the Screenwriting Department Chair, and instructors like David Wheeler and Michael Zam. The school offers several online workshops: "Online 4-Week Screenwriting Workshop" focusing on fundamentals of screenplay structure and format, "Online 8-Week Screenwriting Workshop" providing comprehensive coverage of feature film writing, "Online 12-Week Screenwriting Workshop" offering advanced screenplay development techniques, and "Online 6-Week TV Writing Workshop" specializing in television script formats and series development [1][2].

The Producing School aligns with the professional role supervising the entire film lifecycle including financing, marketing, and distribution. Faculty members include Lydia Cedrone as Department Chair, along with instructors Igor Srubshchik and Fernando Loureiro. Their online workshop offerings include: "Online 4-Week Producing Workshop" covering production fundamentals and project development, "Online 8-Week Producing Workshop" focusing on comprehensive producing strategies from development through distribution, "Online 12-Week Producing Workshop" providing advanced producing techniques including financing and marketing strategies, and "Online 6-Week Documentary Producing Workshop" specializing in non-fiction content production [1][3].

Both schools emphasize practical, hands-on learning approaches through their online platforms, enabling students worldwide to access NYFA's industry-focused curriculum. The workshops are structured to provide progressive skill development, from foundational concepts in shorter programs to comprehensive professional training in extended formats.

## References
[1] https://www.nyfa.edu/
[2] https://www.nyfa.edu/screenwriting-school/
[3] https://www.nyfa.edu/producing/
rounds.answer_b:
Using the 7 Stages of Film Production framework, the two specialized NYFA schools are:

(1) **School of Screenwriting** corresponds to the screenwriter who creates the final Development phase deliverable (the screenplay). The program overview explicitly mentions faculty Keith Dowling (Chair), Jack Rowe, Sean Robinson, and Sonny Calderon[1][2]. Online workshop offerings include: 15-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop, 4-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop, 1-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop, and 12-Week Evening Online Screenwriting Workshop[1][2].

(2) **Producing Department** (within the School of Film and Media Production) aligns with the producer who supervises and manages the entire lifecycle including financing, marketing, and distribution. The program overview explicitly mentions faculty Jack Newman (Chair), Crispin Struthers, Nick Milonas, and Surendra Singh[3][4]. Online workshop offerings include: 15-Week Online Producing Workshop, 4-Week Online Producing Workshop, 1-Week Online Producing Workshop, and 12-Week Evening Online Producing Workshop[3][4].

## References

[2] https://www.nyfa.edu/online-screenwriting-workshops.php
[3] https://www.nyfa.edu/producing.php
[4] https://www.nyfa.edu/online-producing-workshops.php

The two NYFA academic divisions corresponding to these phases are the **Screenwriting Department** led by Dean Nunzio DeFilippis and the **Online MFA in Producing** program chaired by Neal Weisman [1][2].

The Screenwriting Department under Dean DeFilippis focuses on the Development phase of film production through script creation. This department offers three distinct part-time workshop formats: Feature Film Screenwriting workshops that concentrate on three-act structure and character development for theatrical releases, Television Writing workshops covering episodic storytelling for series and streaming platforms, and Documentary Screenwriting workshops focusing on non-fiction narrative structures and research-based storytelling approaches [1][3]. These part-time programs allow students to develop scripts while maintaining professional commitments, with evening and weekend sessions available across multiple formats.

The Online MFA in Producing program led by Chair Neal Weisman addresses the entrepreneurial aspects of filmmaking including financing and marketing phases. The program tuition is $8,333 per semester, with the complete degree requiring four semesters over two years [2][4]. The curriculum includes three key hands-on courses: "Development and Packaging" where students create pitch materials and secure talent attachments, "Film Finance and Distribution" involving practical exercises in budgeting and deal structuring, and "Marketing and Audience Engagement" featuring campaign development and social media strategy implementation [2][5]. Students work on real film projects throughout the program, applying theoretical knowledge to practical producing challenges while building industry networks through virtual collaboration tools and mentorship opportunities.

Both divisions emphasize industry-relevant training, with DeFilippis's screenwriting workshops producing market-ready scripts and Weisman's producing program graduating students equipped to manage all aspects of film financing, marketing, and distribution in today's evolving entertainment landscape.

rounds.answer_b:
The **School of Screenwriting** is led by Dean Nunzio DeFilippis and focuses on the Development phase's script creation. The school offers part-time workshop formats in **Comedy, Horror, and Television Pilot** genres[1][2]. The **Online Master of Arts in Producing** is chaired by Neal Weisman, covering financing and marketing phases. This 36-credit program spans **two years (four semesters)** with a tuition cost of **$14,897 per semester**[3][4]. Hands-on courses include **Line Producing, Production Management, and Pitching, Negotiating & Sales**[3][5].

## References

[3] https://www.nyfa.edu/online-producing-degree.php

[5] https://www.nyfa.edu/producing-courses.php
